title: Moby Dick; Or, The Whale
author: Melville, Herman
summary: Moby-Dick is a sprawling sea narrative centered on whaling, the whale as mythic Leviathan, and a shipbound fate. The narrator frames etymologies, extracts, and encyclopedic cetology, then recounts voyages from Nantucket aboard the Pequod. Captain Ahab dominates as an obsessed harpooner-hunter; a varied crew—Queequeg, Starbuck, Stubb, Flask, and the Parsee Fedallah—populate the deck. Chapters mix practical whaling detail, philosophical digressions, biblical and literary allusion, and encounters with other ships. The text alternates technical description and parable, probing madness, vengeance, and the sea’s enormity while cataloguing whalebone, oil, and cetological lore.
